name: Alex Blake
abstract: Alex Blake (born Alejandro Blake Fearon Jr., December 21, 1951) is a jazz bass player.

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Freddie Hubbard Sextet, the Sonny Rollins Quartet, and the McCoy Tyner Quintet

works.t: People Make the World Go Round

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Freddie Hubbard Sextet, the Sonny Rollins Quartet, and the McCoy Tyner Quintet

works.t: People Make the World Go Round

concerts.label: JVC Jazz Festival: An Evening with The Stan Getz Sextet / Quartet

about_quotes.speaker: Randy Weston
about_quotes.evidence: I had Neil Clarke, Alex Blake; you had Billy Harper, T.K. Blue, and Benny Powell. [...] So, 'Khepera' consisted of connecting China and Africa... I think we had Victor Lewis on drums, Alex Blake on bass, Benny Powell, you know.
about_quotes.doc_title: Randy Weston — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Barron
about_quotes.evidence: Terri Lyne Carrington was playing drums, and Alex Blake playing electric bass and acoustic bass. So that must be the one they are talking about, 1990.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Barron — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Powell
about_quotes.evidence: With our group it’s pretty — this is Randy Weston’s group — ... Alex Blake is the regular bass player with her
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Powell interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 16, 1999 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Paquito D'Rivera
about_quotes.evidence: I wanted Alex Blake to play bass... I asked him, and he recommended Russel Blake.
about_quotes.doc_title: Paquito D'Rivera — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
